summary: This page continues the discussion of partial fraction expansion by showing how an improper rational function includes an added constant term in its decomposition. It presents Example B.11, expanding a rational function into partial fractions, and then introduces modified partial fractions, where terms of the form kx/(x-λ)^r are obtained by first expanding F(x)/x and then multiplying through by x.
keywords: kx over x-minus-lambda to the r
